channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Melbourne Demons Prepare for Talent Scramble with Tasmania
item.description: Melbourne's AFL club, the Demons, have acknowledged their readiness for Tasmania's potential talent poaching, according to coach Steven King. The admission comes amid growing concerns about interstate...

item.title: Nedbank Completes Takeover of NCBA with Excess Share Acceptance
item.description: Nedbank Group's takeover offer for NCBA Group PLC closed on 10 July 2026, with shareholders tendering over 1.3 billion shares—far exceeding the 66% stake initially sought. Valid acceptances covered 79...

item.title: Trump Considers Holding Hegseth Accountable for Iran War Escalation
item.description: As the conflict with Iran intensifies, Donald Trump's frustration is growing, and analysts suggest he may soon seek a scapegoat within his administration. Pete Hegseth, the Defense Secretary, is incre...

item.title: The Importance of Customer Protection in Financial Services
item.description: The article emphasizes the critical role of customer protection in the financial sector, highlighting how safeguarding clients' interests is essential for maintaining trust and ensuring ethical practi...

item.title: FG revives 16 abandoned e-libraries and launches digital hub in Kano
item.description: The Federal Government has revived 16 abandoned digital e-library projects in Federal Unity Colleges as part of efforts to modernize public schools and promote tech-driven learning. Minister of Educat...

item.title: Sleep Country Acquires Sleep Number for $700 Million, Expands U.S. Operations
item.description: Sleep Country Canada ZZZ-T has announced its expansion into the U.S. market through the acquisition of Sleep Number, a Minneapolis-based company, in a deal valued at approximately US$702 million. The ...

item.title: Corporate Philanthropy on Mandela Day: A Call for Structural Change
item.description: The article critiques the performative nature of corporate philanthropy on Mandela Day, using the controversial CEO SleepOut campaign as a case study. It highlights how such initiatives, while well-in...

item.title: Tanzanian Graduates Struggle with Competitive Job Market and Informal Sector Challenges
item.description: Thousands of Tanzanian graduates are entering a job market marked by intense competition and limited formal employment opportunities. Despite a slight drop in the national unemployment rate from 8.9% ...

item.title: Jamaat-i-Islami Launches Nationwide Protest Against Petroleum Levy
item.description: Jamaat-i-Islami Pakistan (JI) emir Hafiz Naeemur Rehman announced a nationwide highway blockade and sit-ins on August 7 as part of the second phase of the party’s anti-petroleum levy and anti-inflatio...

item.title: Latitude Festival 2026 celebrates 20th anniversary with Transgressive Records' curated lineup
item.description: Transgressive Records has unveiled its stage takeover lineup for Latitude Festival 2026, marking the festival's 20th anniversary at Henham Park, Suffolk. The event, running from July 23-26, features a...

item.title: Exploring Red River Gorge: Natural Arches and Scenic Vistas in Kentucky
item.description: Red River Gorge, a federally designated geological area in Eastern Kentucky, offers a unique blend of natural beauty and adventure. Located about an hour east of Lexington, this region is renowned for...

item.title: New Thornhill townhomes designed for families with future needs
item.description: The article discusses a new residential development in Thornhill, Ontario, called The Elms, which targets families planning for long-term growth. Designed by Brightstone Developments, the project offe...

item.title: Gaza's solidarity with Spain amid conflict and celebration
item.description: The Spanish football team's World Cup victory has sparked joyous celebrations in Gaza, where their support for Palestinian rights has resonated deeply. Palestinians gathered in cafes and outdoor scree...

item.title: Scientists Discover New Bacterial Genotypes in Pygmy Sperm Whales Linked to Digestive Issues
item.description: Researchers at Florida Atlantic University's Harbor Branch Oceanographic Institute identified three previously unknown Helicobacter bacterial genotypes in pygmy sperm whales (Kogia breviceps) through ...

item.title: Mumias Voter Files Petition Against MP Peter Salasya Over Alleged Misconduct
item.description: A Mumias East voter, Fred Muka, has filed a constitutional petition at the High Court seeking to have MP Peter Salasya removed from office and compel an independent psychiatric assessment. The petitio...

item.title: Heavy rains cause fatalities and damage in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, authorities issue flood and landslide alerts
item.description: Heavy rains and flash floods have caused at least 12 deaths and 18 injuries across Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP) in the past 48 hours, according to the Provincial Disaster Management Authority (PDMA). The i...

item.title: Spain Celebrates Historic World Cup Victory with Nationwide Parade
item.description: Spain's national football team celebrated their second World Cup win on Monday, July 21, 2026, with a grand parade through Madrid. Over one million fans gathered to witness the celebration, waving red...

item.title: Nigeria's Oil Blocks See Increased Bidding Interest
item.description: The Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC) organized a major commercial bidding event for oil blocks, attracting over 140 companies across 37 assets. The event highlighted Nigeria's...

item.title: Reading & Leeds Festival Removes BBC Introducing Stage After 18 Years, Introducing New Venues
item.description: The BBC Introducing Stage, a platform for emerging artists at the Reading & Leeds Festival, is being removed after 18 years as part of a major overhaul. The decision follows a significant expansion of...

item.title: Andrea Johnson Testifies at Madlanga Inquiry Regarding Allegations of Improper Conduct
